banco_oraciones.oracion: The logo is being changed by the client. Again.
Coffee has been grown here since 1900.
The beans were being dried when it started to rain.
Rice isn't grown in the desert.
The rule has never been broken.
The room isn't being used right now.
Is coffee grown in Colombia? Yes, it is.
Was the window broken by the ball? Yes, it was.
Has the task been finished? No, it hasn't.

interaccion: Kenji: Did you know? Coffee **is grown** in 22 departments of Colombia. I read it in a museum!
Sofía: This logo **was designed** by me... and it **is being changed** by the client. Again.
Ben: My phone **was stolen**! ...No, wait. It **has been found**. It was in my pocket.
Abuela Carmen: In this house, dinner **is served** at seven. The rule **was made** in 1985, and it **has never been broken**.
inicio.definicion_pragmatica: The passive voice puts the spotlight on what receives the action, not on who does it — and it always needs two pieces: the verb 'to be' + a past participle.

tabla.titulo: One sentence through the tenses: coffee + grow
filas: A new blend is being grown this year
This coffee was grown by my grandfather
The beans were being grown organically
Coffee has been grown here since 1900
doer in focus → receiver in focus
Farmers grow coffee → Coffee is grown (by farmers)

ejercicios.pregunta: Right now, the logo ___ by the client.

ejercicios.afirmacion: The passive voice always needs the verb 'to be' + a past participle.
'The window broke by the ball' is a correct passive sentence.

ejercicios.texto: Spanish ___ (speak) in Colombia. — present simple passive
The beans ___ (dry) when it started to rain. — past continuous passive
Coffee ___ (grow) here since 1900. — present perfect passive

ejercicios.texto_con_error: The dinner is serving at seven.
ejercicios.respuesta: The dinner is served at seven.

ejercicios.oracion_base: The client is changing the logo.
ejercicios.respuesta: The logo is being changed by the client.

ejercicios.pregunta: Is coffee grown in Colombia? (short answer)
Was your house built by the Romans? (short answer, No)

guion: Kenji: Welcome to my coffee tour! First fact: coffee was brought to Colombia in the 18th century.
Sofía: Nice fact. Where is it grown today?
Kenji: It is grown in 22 departments. And the best beans are picked by hand, one by one.
Kenji: The beans are dried in the sun, and the coffee is exported to the whole world.
Abuela Carmen: And in this house, it has been prepared the same way since 1985. Cups are on the table!

comprension.pregunta: How are the best beans picked?
When was coffee brought to Colombia?

reading.texto: The old house on the corner has a story. It was built in 1923 by an Italian architect who never saw it finished. For decades, it was used as a school, and thousands of children were taught there — Abuela Carmen was one of them. Then it was abandoned for twenty years. Today, everything is different: the house is being restored by a group of young artists. The walls have been painted, the garden has been replanted, and a new roof is being installed this month. 'Beautiful things are not destroyed,' says Abuela Carmen. 'They are just forgotten for a while.'

preguntas.afirmacion: The house was built by an Italian architect.
It was used as a hospital for decades.
The house is being restored by young artists.
The garden hasn't been replanted yet.
